new_classes.definition_en: An alcoholic beverage distilled from a cereal mash, marketed either in bottles or other containers with an alcoholic strength of 40% vol or more.
A whisky distilled and matured in Scotland, with an alcoholic strength of 40% vol or more, presented in containers of 2 litres or less, with added soda water, falling under CN 2208 30 61.
A quality borne by a whisky indicating that it was distilled and matured in Scotland, as required for classification as Scotch Whisky.
A distillation process applied to a cereal mash to produce whisky, as distinguished from grape- or other fruit-based distillation processes.
A mash prepared from cereal grains, used as the feedstock for whisky distillation.

A quality borne by a whisky-based beverage indicating that soda water has been added, used to discriminate CN 2208 30 61 (Scotch Whisky with soda water in containers ≤ 2 L) from pure Scotch Whisky subheadings.
A quality of a distilled spirit indicating that its alcoholic strength is at least 40% by volume, as required for whisky classification under CN 2208 30.

coverage_explanation:
The axioms cover the main distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 30 61:
1. ✅ Whisky as a distilled alcoholic beverage from cereal mash — captured via WhiskyDistillationProcess with CerealMash feedstock part.
2. ✅ Minimum alcoholic strength of 40% vol — captured via MinAlcohol40VolQuality restriction and decimalRange on alcoholByVolumePercent.
3. ✅ Scotch Whisky: distilled and matured in Scotland — captured via ScotlandOriginQuality.
4. ✅ Added soda water — captured via AddedSodaWaterQuality on ScotchWhisky.
5. ✅ Container ≤ 2 L — captured via SmallContainer restriction (using existing TBox class).
Gap: The maturation/ageing requirement for Scotch Whisky (matured in Scotland) cannot be fully expressed in OWL-DL without introducing a maturation process class, which would be speculative without additional legal text detail. The 'marketed in bottles or other containers' wording is structural/commercial and not further constrainable. No specific regulatory individual (footnote, additional code) was present in the TBox for this subheading, so those cannot be referenced. Coverage is set to 0.85 reflecting these minor gaps.
